description_long: This house features a simple rectangular brick structure with a prominent gable roof constructed from wooden trusses. The roof framing is exposed, revealing a network of beams and cross-bracing, with no visible roofing material installed yet. The brick walls are uniform and light gray, with no visible windows or doors, giving the building a utilitarian appearance. The roof overhangs slightly on both long sides, supported by wooden rafters. The structure appears to be a single-story building, likely in the early stages of construction or renovation. The overall design is functional, with a focus on structural clarity and basic materials.
description: A single-story brick house with an exposed wooden gable roof frame and no visible windows or doors. The design is simple and utilitarian, emphasizing structural elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the gable end of the roof and the solid brick wall with no openings.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the exposed wooden roof trusses and the full length of the brick wall, with no windows or doors.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, showing the gable roof and plain brick wall.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with visible roof framing and uninterrupted brick wall.
attribute_entryway_design: no visible entryway; plain brick wall with no door or decorative features
